title: The Country Reads Back

features.name: old shopfront
features.desc-on-touch: A country store learning to read the archive's files. The goods on its shelves are now indexed by Leif and Katita's logics. A feed-grain sack knows its neighbour's data.

features.name: shed where reading happens
features.desc-on-touch: A weathered rural building where the country's own archive lives — boxes, photographs, tool-marks. The archive watches this mirror archive compare notes with itself.

features.name: fence-line chronicle
features.desc-on-touch: The posts here hold marks from Leif's visits — nail-scratches that spell out file dates, wire twists that map node locations. The country learned to write in hardware.

features.name: ground-reading well
features.desc-on-touch: A water source that has learned to hold the archive's metadata. The country draws from it and reads the nodes' coordinates in the taste of minerals.

features.name: payphone where the country called back
features.desc-on-touch: An old rural telephone that connected to ROOT's first question. The country answered by refusing to hang up. The archive has been listening ever since.

inscription: landscape learning that it has been archived, and speaking back in its own tongue

file.name: Country_Response_Transcript_Unindexed.txt
file.note: The country writes in roads, in weather, in the orientation of sheds. The archive is still learning to read it.

reverie.title: REVERIE — shed where the country began to answer
narr: She sat reading nail-marks like text. Weather had written
there for decades. 'The country is reading us back,' she said,
and showed you how rust speaks in colours the archive can't file.
The shed held both — what was kept and the keeping.

exits.label: back to the study where pages reverse
into the paddock where the country writes itself
down to where landscape becomes stone archive
deeper into the horizon where reading has no end
